metadata.notes: Content overhaul to Wingspan standard — Rogue B, March 2026. Major corrections from official rulebook, BGG FAQ, RulePop interactive rulebook.

key_rulings.text: When you Lead: deploy a card to a location (trigger its deploy ability), then collect the TOP card from a DIFFERENT location and gain that location's bonus — OR draw from the deck and roll the Rising die.
When you Scout: reveal the top card of the deck and place (NOT deploy — no ability triggers) it face-up on any location. Gain that location's bonus.
Deploy triggers the card's deploy ability. Place does NOT. Only the Lead action's Step 1 triggers deploy abilities. Scout and die effects use Place.
The Sovereign token is worth 10 VP at game end. Gained by collecting from Luna.
Each Helium token is worth 3 VP at game end.
Influence at The Institute: most tokens = 4 VP each, second most = 2 VP each, all others = 1 VP each.
At game end, lose 10 VP for each card in your hand beyond 7.
The game ends when any single player meets 2 of the 3 conditions, OR when any combination of 3 conditions are met across all players. Conditions: Fleet at 7+, Influence 7+, Helium 7+.
When you collect from Luna: gain/keep the Sovereign token, advance 1 on Fleet, gain 1 Helium, roll the Rising die, and trigger your House ability.
When collecting during Lead, take only the TOP card from the chosen location — not all cards.

subtopics.title: Components
subtopics.content:
#### Cards
- **112 character cards** (65x100mm), each belonging to 1 of 14 Colors
- Each card shows 3 key elements:
  - **Core value** — the number in the upper-left corner
  - **Deploy ability** — middle text, triggers when you play the card to the board
  - **End-game bonus** — bottom text, scores extra if its condition is met at game end
- 6 reference cards summarizing turn actions and end-game triggers
- 25 Automa cards for solo play

#### The Board
- 1 game board (410x284mm) showing 4 locations: **Jupiter**, **Mars**, **Luna**, and **The Institute**
- Each location has a card stack area and a bonus icon
- **Fleet Track** (positions 0–14) runs along the board edge for progressive VP scoring

#### Tokens
- **60 Helium tokens** — red gem-shaped pieces, worth **3 VP each** at game end
- **60 Influence tokens** (10 per player in 6 colors) — placed at The Institute, scored via tiered majority
- **6 Fleet tokens** — 1 per player, tracks position on the Fleet Track
- **1 Sovereign token** — large ornate token worth **10 VP** at game end; changes hands when someone collects from Luna; triggers your House ability
- **1 Rising token** — crescent-moon first-player marker
- **1 custom Rising die** (22mm) — 6 faces: Jupiter (advance Fleet), Mars (gain Helium), Luna (gain Sovereign), Institute (place Influence), Banish (remove a card from the game), Reveal/Place (flip a deck card onto the board)

#### House Tiles
- Each player draws **1 of 6 House tiles** at setup
- Your House gives an asymmetric ability that triggers whenever you gain the Sovereign token:
  - **Apollo** — take the first AND last turn of the game; when you gain Sovereign, reveal and place the top deck card
  - **Ceres** — start with 6 cards instead of 5; when you gain Sovereign, banish 1 card from a location; must banish 1 card from your hand before scoring
  - **Diana** — when you gain Sovereign, place 1 Influence at The Institute
  - **Jupiter** — when you gain Sovereign, advance 1 extra on the Fleet Track
  - **Mars** — when you gain Sovereign, gain 1 extra Helium
  - **Minerva** — when you gain Sovereign, roll the Rising die for an extra bonus

#### Other Bits
- 1 wolf-head card tray and lid for the deck
- 1 scorepad for end-game tallying
- 2 rulebooks (multiplayer and solo)

subtopics.title: Game Structure & Setup
subtopics.content:
#### How the Game Works
- **Hand-management combo game** — your hand of cards at the end IS your score
- Each card has a core value plus conditional end-game bonuses
- On each turn, you do one of two things:
  - **Lead** — deploy a card from your hand to a location (triggering its ability), then collect a different card and a location bonus
  - **Scout** — reveal a deck card to a location and gain its bonus without giving up anything from your hand
- **Critical rule** — you lose **10 VP for every card beyond 7** in your hand
- The game ends when enough end-game conditions are triggered across the table

#### Setting Up
1. **Shuffle all 112 character cards** into a face-down deck and place it in the card tray.
2. **Place 2 face-up cards** at each of the 4 locations (8 cards total on the board).
3. **Deal 5 cards** to each player as their starting hand (6 if you drew House Ceres).
4. **Each player draws 1 random House tile** and takes their 10 matching Influence tokens.
5. **Place all Fleet tokens** on position 0 of the Fleet Track.
6. **Place the Helium supply** near the board where everyone can reach it.
7. **Place the Sovereign token** beside the board — nobody owns it yet.
8. **Give the Rising token** to the Apollo player (if in play), or choose a starting player randomly.
9. **Place the Rising die** near the board.

#### Checkpoint
- 5 cards in hand (6 for Ceres)
- 10 Influence tokens in your personal supply
- Fleet token on position 0
- House tile in front of you, plus a reference card
- Board shows 2 face-up cards at each of the 4 locations
- Sovereign token and Rising die sit nearby, unclaimed

subtopics.title: Turn Actions: Lead & Scout
subtopics.content:
#### Your Turn
- On your turn, you do exactly **one** of two things: **Lead** or **Scout**

#### Action 1: Lead (Deploy, Then Collect)

1. **Deploy a card** — Pick 1 card from your hand and place it face-up **on top** of any of the 4 locations.
   - That card's **deploy ability** (middle text) triggers immediately
   - Deploy abilities can: draw cards, gain Helium, advance Fleet, place Influence, banish cards, or chain effects
   - If ability conditions aren't met, you can still deploy — the ability simply doesn't resolve

2. **Collect** — Choose ONE of these options:
   - **(a) Collect from a location** — take the **top card** from a **different** location (not where you deployed) into your hand, then gain that location's bonus
   - **(b) Draw from the deck** — draw the top deck card into your hand, then **roll the Rising die** and gain whatever it shows

#### Location Bonuses (When Collecting)
- **Jupiter** — advance your Fleet token **1 space** on the Fleet Track
- **Mars** — gain **1 Helium** from the supply
- **Luna** — the jackpot: gain the **Sovereign token** (take from whoever has it), **+1 Fleet**, **+1 Helium**, a **Rising die roll**, and your **House tile ability** triggers
- **The Institute** — place **1 Influence token** from your supply onto The Institute

#### Action 2: Scout
1. **Reveal** the top card of the deck
2. **Place** it face-up on top of any of the 4 locations — this is a PLACE, not a deploy, so the card's deploy ability does **NOT** trigger
3. **Gain that location's bonus** (same bonuses listed above)

#### Deploy vs. Place — The Critical Distinction
- **Deploy** — your card, from your hand; its deploy ability triggers
- **Place** — a card goes to a location but its ability does NOT trigger
- Only the Lead action's Step 1 deploys. Scout and Rising die effects always place.

subtopics.title: Colors, Die & Resources
subtopics.content:
#### Card Colors — Key Colors
- Every card belongs to 1 of 14 Colors; here are the ones with direct mechanical significance:

**Wild Colors:**
- **Orange** — acts as **wild for character names**; satisfies end-game bonuses requiring a specific named character
- **Gray** — acts as **wild for colors**; satisfies bonuses requiring a card of a specific Color
- Even a single Orange or Gray card can unlock bonus conditions across multiple other cards in your hand

**Track/Location Colors:**
- **Blue** — advances Fleet when deployed; core Color for Jupiter/Fleet strategies
- **Copper** — synergizes with The Institute and Influence; pays off when competing for majority
- **Gold** — highest core values in the game (30–40 range) with powerful end-game bonuses
- **Silver** — specializes in the Helium economy (converting, multiplying, rewarding Helium stockpiles)
- **White** — rewards holding or gaining the Sovereign token

**Other Colors** (Red, Brown, Pink, Obsidian, Yellow, Green, Violet) — thematic abilities like banishing cards, moving cards between locations, cycling the deck, and set-collection bonuses

#### The Rising Die
- Rolled when you draw from the deck during Lead, and whenever Luna or a card effect triggers it
- 6 faces:
  1. **Jupiter** — advance 1 on Fleet Track
  2. **Mars** — gain 1 Helium
  3. **Luna** — gain or keep the Sovereign token (triggers your House ability)
  4. **Institute** — place 1 Influence token
  5. **Banish (X)** — remove the top card from any location entirely from the game
  6. **Reveal/Place** — reveal the top deck card and place it on any location

#### The Sovereign Token
- Worth **10 VP** at game end — one of the biggest individual point sources
- **Gained by** — collecting from Luna, rolling Luna on the die, or certain card abilities; taken from whoever currently holds it
- **House trigger** — every time you gain the Sovereign (even if you already hold it), your House tile ability triggers; repeated Luna visits stack bonuses
- **Tiebreaker** — if final scores are tied, whoever holds the Sovereign wins

#### Fleet Track
- Positions 0–14 with **progressive scoring** printed on the track (0 VP at position 0, up to **43 VP** at position 14)
- **Advance by** — collecting from Jupiter, deploying Blue cards, rolling Jupiter on the die, or collecting from Luna
- Reaching position **7+** is one of the three end-game trigger conditions

#### Influence Tokens
- Start with 10 Influence tokens in your color
- **Place at The Institute** via location bonus, card abilities, or the Rising die
- **Tiered scoring** at game end: most Influence = **4 VP/token**, second most = **2 VP/token**, all others = **1 VP/token**
- Having **7+ Influence** at The Institute is one of the three end-game trigger conditions

#### Helium
- Each token worth **3 VP** at game end
- **Gained from** — Mars, Luna, card abilities, or the Rising die
- Some Silver cards convert Helium into other benefits
- Reaching **7+ Helium** tokens is one of the three end-game trigger conditions

subtopics.title: Endgame & Scoring
subtopics.content:
#### End-Game Triggers
- The game ends when **either** of these happens:
  - **Any single player** meets **2 of the 3 conditions**, OR
  - **All 3 conditions** are met by **any combination of players** (different players can satisfy different conditions)
- The three conditions share the same threshold of **7**:
  1. **7+ Helium tokens**
  2. **7+ Influence tokens** at The Institute
  3. **Fleet token** at position **7+** on the Fleet Track
- Once triggered, **all players complete the current round** so everyone gets equal turns
- If House Apollo is in play, that player takes the final turn
- **No deck-exhaustion trigger** — only Helium, Influence, and Fleet can end the game

#### Scoring Formula
1. **Card core values** — sum the upper-left number on every card in your hand
2. **Card end-game bonuses** — check each card's bottom text; score bonuses for conditions met; some cards penalize if conditions are NOT met
3. **Fleet Track** — VP based on position (0 at position 0, up to 43 at position 14)
4. **Helium** — **3 VP** per token
5. **Sovereign bonus** — **10 VP** if you hold the Sovereign token
6. **Influence at The Institute** — tiered: most = **4 VP/token**, second most = **2 VP/token**, all others = **1 VP/token**
7. **Excess card penalty** — **-10 VP** per card beyond 7 in your hand

#### Tiebreaker
- If scores are tied, the player holding the Sovereign token wins.

subtopics.title: Beginner Strategy
subtopics.content:
#### Core Principle: Hand Quality Over Quantity
- **Aim for 5–7 high-synergy cards**, not a pile of 10 mediocre ones
- At **-10 VP per card beyond 7**, a bloated hand is devastating
- If your hand grows past 7, deploy your weakest cards immediately

#### Where the Big Points Live
- **Gold cards** — highest core values (30–40 range); even one anchors your entire score
- **Fleet Track** — up to 43 VP; aggressive Jupiter and Blue strategies are among the strongest
- **Sovereign token** — flat 10 VP, plus triggers your House ability each time you gain it
- **Helium** — 3 VP each; five tokens = 15 VP
- **Influence** — tiered scoring; MOST tokens at The Institute = 4 VP each vs. 1 VP each for everyone else

#### Orange and Gray: Your Best Support Colors
- **Orange** — wild for character names; satisfies end-game bonuses requiring specific characters
- **Gray** — wild for colors; satisfies conditions requiring specific Colors
- Even one Orange or Gray can unlock bonus conditions across multiple cards in your hand

#### House-Color Synergies
- **House Jupiter + Blue cards** — Blues advance Fleet on deploy; Jupiter's ability adds +1 Fleet when you gain Sovereign
- **House Diana + Copper cards** — Diana places Influence when you gain Sovereign; Copper synergizes with The Institute majority
- **House Mars + Silver cards** — Mars gains extra Helium when you gain Sovereign; Silvers convert and multiply Helium
- **House Apollo + White cards** — Apollo gets first and last turns; Whites reward holding the Sovereign

#### When to Lead vs. Scout
- **Lead** — usually the stronger play; you gain a location bonus AND a new card, at the cost of deploying one card
- **Scout** — shines when your hand is already strong (5–6 great cards) and you don't want to risk giving one up
- **Scouting to Luna** — especially powerful; you get Sovereign, Fleet, Helium, a die roll, and your House ability without losing a card

#### Watching End-Game Triggers
- The game ends when 1 player hits 2 of 3 conditions, or all 3 conditions are met across any combination of players
- **Don't trigger the end before you're ready** — if your hand still needs work, avoid pushing past 7 on any track
- **If an opponent is close to triggering** — either race to finish your combos or slow their path by competing for the same resources

#### Common Mistakes
- **Holding 8+ cards** — each card beyond 7 costs 10 VP; deploy your weakest cards aggressively
- **Deploying Gold cards** — a 35-point Gold deployed for 3 VP in Helium is a 32-VP loss; always deploy lowest-value cards
- **Ignoring the Sovereign token** — 10 VP is massive; if nobody contests Luna, Scout there repeatedly
- **Spreading across all 14 Colors** — focus on 2–3 Colors that synergize with your House tile; use Orange and Gray as wild support
- **Confusing deploy and place** — Scout and die effects PLACE cards (no abilities trigger); only Lead Step 1 deploys

subtopics.title: First-Game Walkthrough
subtopics.content:
#### Your First Game of Red Rising

**After Setup**: You have 5 cards in hand (6 if House Ceres), a House tile showing your Sovereign ability, 10 Influence tokens, and your Fleet token at position 0. Each location has 2 face-up cards. The Sovereign token is unclaimed.

#### Opening Turns

**Turn 1 — Lead**:
1. Find the weakest card in your hand (lowest core value or impossible bonus)
2. Deploy it to a location (e.g., Luna) — its deploy ability triggers, so resolve it
3. Collect from a different location — if Mars has a Gold card on top, take it and gain 1 Helium from Mars's bonus
4. You just traded a weak card for a strong one and picked up a resource

**Turn 2 — Scout to Luna**:
1. Reveal the top deck card and place it on Luna (placing does NOT trigger deploy abilities)
2. Collect Luna's full bonus package: Sovereign token (10 VP), +1 Fleet, +1 Helium, Rising die roll, and your House ability
3. All of that without giving up a single card

**Turns 3–5 — Build Your Combos**:
- Keep deploying your weakest cards and collecting stronger ones from locations
- Watch for **Orange** and **Gray** cards — they act as wilds for end-game bonus conditions
- Keep your hand at or below 7 cards to dodge the -10 VP penalty

#### Mid-Game
- Check which end-game conditions are approaching 7 (Fleet, Influence, Helium)
- **If your combos are ready** — push tracks to trigger the end
- **If your hand needs work** — slow down and keep sculpting instead of pushing tracks

#### Late Game — Lock In Your Score
- Stop deploying anything valuable; Scout if your hand is strong
- Verify your end-game bonus conditions are met — check whether Orange and Gray wilds complete requirements
- Count your hand size and deploy down to 7 if needed

#### End-Game Trigger
- Game ends when 1 player hits 2 of 3 conditions (Fleet 7+, Influence 7+, Helium 7+), or when all 3 are met across any combination of players
- No deck-exhaustion trigger
- Everyone finishes the current round for equal turns

#### Scoring
- **Add**: card core values + end-game bonuses + Fleet Track VP + (Helium x 3) + Sovereign (10 VP) + Influence (tiered: 4/2/1 VP per token)
- **Subtract**: 10 VP per card beyond 7 in your hand
- First-game target: **80–120 VP** is competitive

subtopics.title: Advanced Strategy
subtopics.content:
#### Hand Sculpting
- Every deploy-and-collect decision should ask: "Does this improve my end-game hand?"
- **Deploy ceiling** — never deploy a card worth more than the benefit you gain; deploying a 35-point Gold for 3 VP Helium is a 32-VP loss
- **Hand trimming** — at 8 cards you eat -10 VP, at 9 that's -20; actively deploy down to 7 or below

#### Color Synergy Stacking
- **Gold + Orange/Gray** — Gold has highest core values (30–40) with demanding conditions; Orange (wild names) and Gray (wild colors) make those conditions dramatically easier
- **Blue + Jupiter location** — Blue advances Fleet on deploy; combine with Jupiter collections for rapid advancement; Fleet 10+ is worth 25+ VP
- **Copper + Institute majority** — Copper synergizes with Influence; majority = 4 VP/token (20–24 VP from 5–6 tokens) vs. 1 VP/token at the bottom tier
- **Silver + Helium stacking** — Silver converts and multiplies Helium; pair with House Mars for 8–10 Helium (24–30 VP)

#### The Sovereign Token Economy
- **Luna gives you everything** — Sovereign (10 VP), +1 Fleet, +1 Helium (3 VP), a die roll, and your House ability; potentially 15+ VP from a single collection
- **House abilities compound** — House Jupiter gets +1 Fleet each gain, House Mars gets +1 Helium, House Diana places Influence; 3–4 Sovereign gains stacks serious points
- **If nobody contests Luna, Scout there every turn** — all bonuses without losing a card; one of the strongest repeatable plays

#### Location Mastery
- **Jupiter** — advance Fleet; go-to for Blue-heavy strategies or racing toward Fleet 7+ trigger
- **Mars** — gain Helium (3 VP each); best for Silver strategies or Helium 7+ trigger
- **Luna** — the full package; best overall but highly contested in multiplayer
- **The Institute** — place Influence; best for Copper strategies chasing majority; less impactful if behind in the race

#### End-Game Trigger Manipulation
- **Trigger when ahead** — if your hand scores 120+ and opponents are still building, push past 7 on two tracks to force the end
- **Stall when behind** — avoid advancing past 7; let opponents trigger and you still finish the round
- **Watch the collective threshold** — all 3 conditions met by any combination of players ends the game, even if no single player has 2

#### Player Count Adjustments
- **2–3 players** — locations stay stacked; Sovereign changes hands less often; engine strategies have more time
- **4–6 players** — locations get stripped quickly; Luna is fiercely contested; end-game triggers fire faster; prioritize hand quality early
